video_title: Guy Fieri Feasts On German Comfort Food in Arizona 🇩🇪  | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: House Murphy's
restaurants.guy_intro: And that brings us here to Glendale, Arizona, to House Murphy's, where Rose Hoffman grew up on the kind of food made here in Munich, and her husband, Brett, learned from his German grandparents here in Arizona.

dishes.description: A sweet and sour pot roast made from a big top round of beef, trimmed and marinated for 4-5 days in vinegar, buttermilk, mirepoix, and spices. The meat is then browned, deglazed with red wine, cooked down with the marinade, strained, and the sauce is thickened with a cornstarch slurry, seasoned with kitchen bouquet, white pepper, salt, brown sugar, and raisins.
dishes.guy_response: That is great. It's not too tender. It's not too tough. But there is a contrast. A sweet... And sour. Mm-hmm. Do you like the sweet and the sour? I do like the sweet and the sour. It's very good. Delicious. Can't get anything better than this. Real German food.

restaurants.name: Beer Garden
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here on the edge of Koreatown in Los Angeles, checking out Beer Garden, a Fusion Grill and craft beer spot, where they've got a Korean-German combination that's food you literally can't get anywhere else.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in Korean beer, water, honey, parsley, lemon, garlic, bay leaf, oregano, salt, and pepper. It's then patted dry, seasoned, and roasted in the oven with Guinness beer in the cavity. Served with cabbage frisee salad, pickled jalapeno, and pickled daikon.
dishes.guy_response: We like to do beer can chicken, but I don't think I've ever had one that's infused and has this much flavor of beer. You get some of that. Not bad. Nice roasted flavor from the skin. Then you get a little bite of that slaw. Go buy that jalapeno. Chef, nicely done.

restaurants.name: Lauer Kraut
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm about 20 miles north of Denver, Colorado, in a little farming community called Brighton to check out a joint that's serving up fourth-generation German family recipes and something called a kraut burger. Now, if you're expecting a burger with a bunch of kraut on top, ho, ho, ho, you got another thing coming. This is Lauer Kraut.

dishes.description: A homemade bread pocket filled with a mixture of 80-20 ground beef, onions, salt, black pepper, and homemade sauerkraut (made from hand-shredded cabbage, salted, mashed, and fermented for 28 days). The filling is cooked, cooled, then added to the dough, sealed, and baked. Optionally made with pepper jack or American cheese.
dishes.guy_response: It's like a meat pretzel. This is crazy. The pepper. Ugh. Prime time. The nice tender bun. It's moist. It's soft. It's doughy. It's pretzel-like. I'm going to try one with cheese. Mmm. Oh, yeah. Where have these been all my life? Well, I'm glad you like it. Nice job. Thank you. Congratulations. Sauerkraut original. Oh, it's just wonderful. The cabbage and the meat is fabulous. It's fresh. The cabbage is what makes it delicious.

restaurants.name: Bohemian Beer Garden
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in beautiful Boulder, Colorado. I love this town. So when you get good times, you get awesome people, what do you need? You need a beer garden. Not just any beer garden. One that really makes you feel like you're transported back to another country. Try it here. Bohemian Beer Garden.

dishes.description: A dessert with a filling of Granny Smith apples, butter, salt, cinnamon, allspice, nutmeg, sugar, golden raisins (soaked in light rum), toasted walnuts, graham cracker crumbs, cornstarch slurry, and lemon. The cooled filling is rolled into a log, wrapped in puff pastry, egg washed, coated with sugar, and baked. Served with Creme Anglaise, salted caramel, candy walnuts, and whipped cream.
dishes.guy_response: I've been turned onto the streusel. That's really nice. I have this really tart creme sauce and the caramel all comes together. It's delicious. The apples are cooked perfectly. There's still a little bit of bite to them. You still get a little bit of that tartness. You don't typically see strudel that has this much filling. That's all thriller, not filler. Exactly. Well done. We have the apple strudel. Outside is very flaky and crispy. A nice apple filling. Nice apple flavor. Not too sweet. Really well balanced. A giant addition to Mulder.

restaurants.name: Bridget and Bernard's Garden Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: So, I'm here in Maui in a not-so-traditional place looking for a, well, not-so-traditional restaurant. But you're looking for some German food. And you're here at Bridget and Bernard's Garden Cafe.

dishes.description: A traditional German-Swiss dish made with thin slices of eye of round beef, doctored with Dijon mustard, raw smoked bacon, kosher dill pickle, and caramelized onions. Rolled tight, seasoned, oven-seared, then simmered in red wine and jus. Served with spaetzle and braised red cabbage.
dishes.guy_response: The meat really cooks down to where it's almost like shredded pot roast. There's a nice crust on the outside of it. Caramelized onions are awesome. You need the acidity of the dill pickle. A little bit of touch of the mustard, of course. The cabbage is the perfect complement. We've had a lot of German food. I was in Munich. I would still be in Munich if I had had this. What a nice dish, chef. It's so delicious. I grew up in a German family in Detroit. And it shocked me to find rouladen so great and on Maui.

dishes.description: Homemade noodles made from water, egg, oil, nutmeg, paprika, and unbleached flour. Pressed through a spaetzle maker into boiling water, then transferred to cold water. Served with butter and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: I've seen a lot of spaetzle a lot of ways. This might be one of my favorite. The spaetzle is stupid. I've just come to order a side of spaetzle.

dishes.description: An old European dish made with red cabbage, onions, garlic, bay leaf, nutmeg, sugar, clove, white vinegar, red wine, and water, cooked down for 45 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: The cabbage is the perfect complement.
